articles.title: A UFO Whistleblower died of an "accidental overdose" months before he was set to testify to congress and now the FBI is now investigating
articles.summary: Matthew James Sullivan, a 39‑year‑old Air Force veteran who had worked for the Air Force Intelligence Agency, the National Air and Space Intelligence Center and the NSA, was found dead in his Falls Church, Virginia home on May 12 2024; the medical examiner ruled his death an accidental overdose of alcohol, alprazolam, cyclobenzaprine and imipramine. Sullivan had been scheduled to testify before Congress in November 2024 about the government’s secret UFO crash‑retrieval program and had personally seen UFOs in federal possession. Representative Eric Burlison sent a letter to FBI Director Kash Patel expressing concern that Sullivan’s sudden death raised “substantial questions” and potential foul play, citing national‑security implications. The FBI responded that it is “spearheading the effort to look for connections into the missing and deceased scientists” and is working with the Department of Energy, the Department of War, and state and local law‑enforcement partners. Retired Major General David Abba, who directed the Pentagon’s special access programs, said at Sullivan’s funeral that Sullivan “held the burden that a select few in this nation have of truly understanding what’s going on.” The article notes a pattern of other whistleblowers—such as David Grusch, David Wilcock and Amy Eskridge—who have died under circumstances the authorities have ruled accidental or self‑inflicted.

de.title: Ein UFO-Whistleblower starb an einer „versehentlichen Überdosis“, Monate bevor er vor dem Kongress aussagen sollte, und jetzt ermittelt das FBI
de.summary: Matthew James Sullivan, ein 39-jähriger Air-Force-Veteran, der für die Air Force Intelligence Agency, das National Air and Space Intelligence Center und die NSA gearbeitet hatte, wurde am 12. Mai 2024 tot in seinem Haus in Falls Church, Virginia, aufgefunden; Der Gerichtsmediziner entschied, dass sein Tod eine versehentliche Überdosis Alkohol, Alprazolam, Cyclobenzaprin und Imipramin sei. Sullivan sollte im November 2024 vor dem Kongress über das geheime UFO-Absturz-Bergungsprogramm der Regierung aussagen und hatte persönlich UFOs in Bundesbesitz gesehen. Vertreter Eric Burlison schickte einen Brief an FBI-Direktor Kash Patel, in dem er seine Besorgnis darüber zum Ausdruck brachte, dass Sullivans plötzlicher Tod „erhebliche Fragen“ und mögliches Fehlverhalten aufwirft, und verwies auf Auswirkungen auf die nationale Sicherheit. Das FBI antwortete, dass es „an der Spitze der Bemühungen steht, nach Verbindungen zu den vermissten und verstorbenen Wissenschaftlern zu suchen“ und mit dem Energieministerium, dem Kriegsministerium sowie staatlichen und lokalen Strafverfolgungspartnern zusammenarbeitet. Der pensionierte Generalmajor David Abba, der die Sonderzugangsprogramme des Pentagons leitete, sagte bei Sullivans Beerdigung, dass Sullivan „die Bürde trug, die einige wenige in dieser Nation haben, wirklich zu verstehen, was ist.“ geht weiter.“ Der Artikel weist auf ein Muster bei anderen Whistleblowern hin – etwa David Grusch, David Wilcock und Amy Eskridge –, die unter Umständen gestorben sind, die die Behörden als versehentlich oder selbstverschuldet eingestuft haben.

articles.title: Pentagon’s UAP Data Playbook Quietly Goes Public—And It May Reveal How Disclosure Will Actually Unfold
articles.summary: A DoD‑sponsored workshop report titled “2025 UAP Workshop: Narrative Data, Infrastructures, and Analysis” was cleared for open publication on February 11, 2026 and posted to the AARO website without fanfare. The report, produced by the All‑Domain Anomaly Resolution Office, summarizes a two‑day meeting in August 2025 that brought together 40 participants from government agencies, universities, and independent research organizations. Its mandate was to design a permanent science infrastructure for studying UAP at scale, not to debate whether UAP exist. Key findings include that UAP data is fragmented across military logs, radar archives, social media, and other sources; classification is the biggest barrier; AI and machine learning are being explored but carry risks of hallucination and bias; pilot reporting stigma suppresses timely data; and fake data and AI‑generated hoaxes threaten research integrity. The workshop recommends modular metadata standards, interface control documents and APIs, a unified security solution for classified and unclassified data, and benchmarking systems, and it proposes redesigning the public reporting portal to capture free‑text descriptions, AI‑extracted structured data, and links to FAA flight tracks, weather radar, and astronomical databases. The document indicates that the U.S. government is treating UAP as a serious, data‑rich phenomenon requiring permanent scientific infrastructure and is preparing for a large‑scale public disclosure event.

de.title: Das UAP-Daten-Playbook des Pentagon wird stillschweigend veröffentlicht – und es könnte enthüllen, wie sich die Offenlegung tatsächlich entwickeln wird
de.summary: Ein vom DoD gesponserter Workshop-Bericht mit dem Titel „2025 UAP Workshop: Narrative Data, Infrastructures, and Analysis“ wurde am 11. Februar 2026 zur offenen Veröffentlichung freigegeben und ohne großes Aufsehen auf der AARO-Website veröffentlicht. Der vom All-Domain Anomaly Resolution Office erstellte Bericht fasst ein zweitägiges Treffen im August 2025 zusammen, an dem 40 Teilnehmer von Regierungsbehörden, Universitäten und unabhängigen Forschungsorganisationen teilnahmen. Sein Auftrag bestand darin, eine permanente wissenschaftliche Infrastruktur für die Untersuchung von UAP in großem Maßstab zu entwerfen, und nicht darin, darüber zu diskutieren, ob UAP existiert. Zu den wichtigsten Erkenntnissen gehört, dass UAP-Daten über Militärprotokolle, Radararchive, soziale Medien und andere Quellen fragmentiert sind; Die Klassifizierung ist das größte Hindernis. KI und maschinelles Lernen werden erforscht, bergen jedoch das Risiko von Halluzinationen und Voreingenommenheit; Das Stigma der Pilotenberichterstattung unterdrückt aktuelle Daten. und gefälschte Daten und KI-generierte Falschmeldungen bedrohen die Integrität der Forschung. Der Workshop empfiehlt modulare Metadatenstandards, Schnittstellenkontrolldokumente und APIs, eine einheitliche Sicherheitslösung für klassifizierte und nicht klassifizierte Daten sowie Benchmarking-Systeme und schlägt eine Neugestaltung des öffentlichen Berichtsportals zur Erfassung von Freitextbeschreibungen vor. KI-extrahierte strukturierte Daten und Links zu FAA-Flugspuren, Wetterradar und astronomischen Datenbanken. Aus dem Dokument geht hervor, dass die US-Regierung UAP als ernstes, datenreiches Phänomen betrachtet, das eine permanente wissenschaftliche Infrastruktur erfordert, und sich auf eine groß angelegte Offenlegungsveranstaltung vorbereitet.

articles.title: Would-be UFO whistleblower died of accidental drug overdose after agreeing to testify to Congress
articles.summary: Matthew James Sullivan, a 39‑year‑old Air Force veteran, died on May 12, 2024 at his home in Falls Church, Va., from an accidental drug overdose involving alcohol, alprazolam, cyclobenzaprine and imipramine, according to the Northern District Office of the Chief Medical Examiner. Sullivan had agreed to testify before Congress about secret government UFO programs and was preparing to appear at a hearing scheduled for November 2024. Representative Eric Burlison (R‑Mo.) expressed grave concern over Sullivan’s death, citing “implications for national security,” and referred the case to the FBI in a letter to FBI Director Kash Patel. The FBI said it could be investigating Sullivan’s death as part of a broader effort to examine the circumstances surrounding the disappearance or death of a dozen other U.S. scientists, working with the Department of Energy, Department of War and local law enforcement. Sullivan had earned a Bronze Star for valor in Operation Enduring Freedom, worked for the Air Force Intelligence Agency, the National Air and Space Intelligence Center and the NSA, and was described by retired Major General David Abba as carrying “the burden that a select few in this nation have of truly understanding what’s going on.” Other UFO whistleblowers, such as David Grusch, have reported threats and reprisals after revealing evidence of U.S. possession of UFOs, and Grusch’s 2022 letter to the Intelligence Community Office of Inspector General included information about Sullivan’s death, though the IC OIG has stated it cannot confirm or deny any ongoing investigations.

Das FBI sagte, es könnte Sullivans Tod im Rahmen einer umfassenderen Anstrengung untersuchen, um die Umstände des Verschwindens oder Todes eines Dutzend anderer US-Wissenschaftler zu untersuchen und dabei mit dem Energieministerium, dem Kriegsministerium und den örtlichen Strafverfolgungsbehörden zusammenzuarbeiten. Sullivan hatte einen Bronze Star für seine Tapferkeit bei der Operation Enduring Freedom erhalten, arbeitete für die Air Force Intelligence Agency, das National Air and Space Intelligence Center und die NSA und wurde vom pensionierten Generalmajor David Abba als jemand beschrieben, der „die Last trägt, die einige wenige in dieser Nation tragen“. Ich muss wirklich verstehen, was vor sich geht.“ Andere UFO-Whistleblower wie David Grusch haben von Drohungen und Repressalien berichtet, nachdem sie Beweise für den Besitz von UFOs durch die USA enthüllt hatten, und Gruschs Brief aus dem Jahr 2022 an das Intelligence Community Office of Inspector General enthielt Informationen über Sullivans Tod, obwohl das IC OIG erklärt hat, dass es laufende Ermittlungen weder bestätigen noch dementieren kann.

articles.title: Multiple Pastors Claim U.S. Military Intelligence Officers Secretly Briefed Them To Prepare Their Congregations For UAP Disclosure
articles.summary: Several religious leaders, including Pastor Perry Stone and evangelist Tony Merkel, claim they were privately briefed by U.S. military intelligence officers to prepare their congregations for an upcoming UAP disclosure. Stone, a Tennessee‑based Pentecostal evangelist, said in a video that a trusted associate informed him that a group of pastors was invited to hear directly from government‑connected individuals about an imminent disclosure event involving unidentified anomalous phenomena. Stone described the briefing as covering craft not allegedly part of our planet, non‑human beings, and a coordinated propaganda plan already in motion. Merkel says he has been in contact with officials for over a year, describing them as Christians in intelligence operations assigned to work within the UAP disclosure community, and that they approached him with a mission to prepare the body of Christ for what is coming. The state where the briefing took place has not been disclosed, and no government memo, official invitation, transcript, or attendee list has surfaced. The White House has not denied the claims, and Washington has remained silent, a silence the article notes is significant.

de.title: Mehrere Pastoren behaupten, US-Militärgeheimdienstoffiziere hätten sie heimlich informiert, um ihre Gemeinden auf die UAP-Offenlegung vorzubereiten
de.summary: Mehrere religiöse Führer, darunter Pastor Perry Stone und der Evangelist Tony Merkel, behaupten, sie seien privat von US-Militärgeheimdienstoffizieren informiert worden, um ihre Gemeinden auf eine bevorstehende Offenlegung der UAP vorzubereiten. Stone, ein in Tennessee ansässiger Pfingstevangelist, sagte in einem Video, ein vertrauenswürdiger Mitarbeiter habe ihn darüber informiert, dass eine Gruppe von Pastoren eingeladen worden sei, direkt von Personen mit Regierungskontakten über eine bevorstehende Offenlegung unbekannter anomaler Phänomene zu hören. Stone beschrieb das Briefing so, dass es sich um Raumfahrzeuge handelte, die angeblich nicht Teil unseres Planeten sind, nichtmenschliche Lebewesen und einen koordinierten Propagandaplan, der bereits umgesetzt wurde. Merkel sagt, er stehe seit über einem Jahr mit Beamten in Kontakt und beschreibe sie als Christen in Geheimdienstoperationen, die innerhalb der Offenlegungsgemeinschaft der UAP arbeiten sollen, und dass sie mit der Mission an ihn herangetreten seien, den Leib Christi auf das Kommende vorzubereiten. Der Staat, in dem das Briefing stattfand, wurde nicht bekannt gegeben, und es sind weder ein Regierungsmemorandum noch eine offizielle Einladung, ein Protokoll oder eine Teilnehmerliste aufgetaucht. Das Weiße Haus hat die Behauptungen nicht bestritten und Washington hat geschwiegen, ein Schweigen, das dem Artikel zufolge bedeutsam ist.

articles.title: ‘SLEEPING DOG’ Trailer Dives Into Jeremy Corbell’s Fight For UAP Disclosure
articles.summary: The trailer for “SLEEPING DOG” offers a first look at a documentary that follows investigative journalist and filmmaker Jeremy Corbell and his controversial rise in the UAP disclosure movement. Directed by Michael Lazovsky, the film has been described as a real‑life “Disclosure Day” and charts Corbell’s transition from mixed martial artist to a prominent figure pushing for transparency around unidentified aerial phenomena. It will open in select theaters on May 8, 2026, before becoming available on digital platforms on May 12. The documentary explores Corbell’s long partnership with investigative reporter George Knapp and their attempts to bring military‑filmed UAP footage and whistleblower testimony to the public eye. It promises unprecedented access and never‑before‑seen footage from Corbell’s earliest investigations, featuring figures such as astronaut Edgar Mitchell, John Lear, Bob Lazar, David Grusch, David Fravor, John B. Alexander and others involved in the UAP transparency movement. The film is produced by Jeremy Corbell, Jim Martin and Michael Lazovsky and aims to provide an intimate, high‑stakes look at one of the most polarizing subjects of the modern era.

articles.title: The 2023 Las Vegas Backyard UFO Case Returns To Center Stage As Washington Moves Toward Disclosure
articles.summary: On May 1, 2023 a streaking light was reported over the Southwest, and the American Meteor Society recorded sightings in Nevada, California, Arizona, and Utah. A Las Vegas family called 911 shortly afterward, claiming a crash in their backyard and describing large non‑human beings that were 8 to 10 feet tall, thin, and greenish. Police responded, body‑cam footage was captured, and the family appeared visibly shaken, while other witnesses reported a sky event. Crime‑scene reconstruction analyst Scott Roder performed a frame‑by‑frame review of the backyard video and publicly asserted that the footage showed at least two beings using a “cloaking” effect, concluding the entities were present in the real‑world environment and inviting peer review. Police responded, body‑cam footage was captured, and the family appeared visibly shaken, while other witnesses reported a sky event. Crime‑scene reconstruction analyst Scott Roder performed a frame‑by‑frame review of the backyard video and publicly asserted that the footage showed at least two beings using a “cloaking” effect, concluding the entities were present in the real‑world environment and inviting peer review. President Donald Trump announced that UFO files would be released “very, very soon,” and members of Congress have begun openly discussing UAPs, non‑human intelligence, interdimensional theories, and the Book of Enoch. The article argues that the Las Vegas case should be examined through a legal‑forensic review rather than dismissed or accepted on blind belief.

de.title: Der Hinterhof-UFO-Fall 2023 in Las Vegas rückt wieder in den Mittelpunkt, während Washington sich der Offenlegung nähert

Die Polizei reagierte, es wurden Bodycam-Aufnahmen gemacht und die Familie wirkte sichtlich erschüttert, während andere Zeugen von einem Vorfall am Himmel berichteten. Scott Roder, Analyst für die Rekonstruktion des Tatorts, führte eine Bild-für-Bild-Überprüfung des Hinterhofvideos durch und behauptete öffentlich, dass das Filmmaterial mindestens zwei Lebewesen zeigte, die einen „Tarneffekt“ nutzten. Präsident Donald Trump kündigte an, dass UFO-Dateien „sehr, sehr bald“ veröffentlicht würden, und Kongressabgeordnete haben begonnen, offen über UAPs, nichtmenschliche Intelligenz, interdimensionale Theorien und das Buch Henoch zu diskutieren. Die Bundesregierung steht zunehmend unter Druck, UAP-Aufzeichnungen offenzulegen, doch die UAP-Studie der NASA und der AARO-Bericht haben keine schlüssigen Beweise für außerirdischen Ursprung oder nachweisbare außerirdische Aktivitäten gefunden. articles.title: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna Frames UFO Disclosure Through The Book of Enoch As A Window Into A Reality Beyond Human Perception
articles.summary: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna of Florida publicly suggested that the ongoing discussion about unidentified aerial phenomena may be closely connected to themes found in the Book of Enoch. She frames this link through the interdimensional hypothesis and Carl Sagan’s Flatland teaching model, arguing that both the modern UAP conversation and the ancient text describe interactions between different layers of existence. The UAP conversation, acknowledged by NASA and the Department of Defense, includes documented observations of sudden acceleration, seamless trans‑medium travel, and behavior that does not align with known propulsion systems or aerodynamics. Luna’s remarks emphasize that the Book of Enoch is not presented as scientific evidence but as an early attempt to describe encounters that lacked a clear vocabulary, mirroring the structural parallels of the interdimensional hypothesis. She does not claim that UAPs are definitively linked to the Watchers of the Book of Enoch, noting that no verified evidence currently supports such a connection. The article warns that introducing religious or historical texts into a policy‑driven discussion carries risks of misinterpretation and speculation, while acknowledging that the disclosure process is revealing complexity rather than simple answers.

articles.title: UFO Files Delivered for Public Release, FBI Director Kash Patel Confirms — Here's What to Expect
articles.summary: FBI Director Kash Patel confirmed on 6 May that the FBI has delivered its first batch of classified UFO documents to a Pentagon‑led inter‑agency committee for public release, and that the documents will be released very soon. Patel said the effort is being directed by the Department of War under Secretary Pete Hegseth across all intelligence agencies, following President Donald Trump’s February 2026 executive order to release records on unidentified anomalous phenomena and extraterrestrial matters. Trump had previously teased the release at a Turning Point USA event in Arizona, claiming the government had found “many very interesting documents.” Representative Anna Paulina Luna ordered the Pentagon to hand over 46 specific UAP video clips by 14 April 2026, but the deadline passed with no public confirmation that the videos were delivered. UFO whistleblower David Grusch warned that government forces are still working to cover up the files before release, believes disclosure will reach a tipping point in 60–90 days, and argues the focus should be on physical materials rather than videos. A NewsNation/Decision Desk HQ poll in April 2025 found 44 % of Americans believe the government is hiding UFO information, while a Gallup poll from 1–15 April 2026 reported that 55 % of Americans say their financial situation is getting worse, the highest level since 2001. Patel said he has no information on whether the US has recovered alien bodies from crashed UFOs, and the Pentagon’s All‑Domain Anomaly Resolution Office has investigated hundreds of new cases but has not confirmed extraterrestrial evidence.

de.title: UFO-Dateien zur Veröffentlichung übergeben, bestätigt FBI-Direktor Kash Patel – Folgendes ist zu erwarten
de.summary: FBI-Direktor Kash Patel bestätigte am 6. Mai, dass das FBI seinen ersten Stapel geheimer UFO-Dokumente einem vom Pentagon geführten behördenübergreifenden Ausschuss zur Veröffentlichung übergeben hat und dass die Dokumente sehr bald veröffentlicht werden. Patel sagte, die Bemühungen würden vom Kriegsministerium unter Minister Pete Hegseth in allen Geheimdiensten geleitet und folgten der Anordnung von Präsident Donald Trump vom Februar 2026, Aufzeichnungen über nicht identifizierte anomale Phänomene und außerirdische Angelegenheiten zu veröffentlichen. Trump hatte die Veröffentlichung zuvor bei einer Turning Point USA-Veranstaltung in Arizona angepriesen und behauptet, die Regierung habe „viele sehr interessante Dokumente“ gefunden. Die Vertreterin Anna Paulina Luna befahl dem Pentagon, bis zum 14. April 2026 46 spezifische UAP-Videoclips zu übergeben, doch die Frist verstrich, ohne dass eine öffentliche Bestätigung der Lieferung der Videos erfolgte. Der UFO-Whistleblower David Grusch warnte, dass die Regierung noch immer daran arbeite, die Akten vor der Veröffentlichung zu vertuschen. Eine Umfrage von NewsNation/Decision Desk HQ im April 2025 ergab, dass 44 % der Amerikaner an die Regierung glauben versteckt UFO-Informationen, während eine Gallup-Umfrage vom 1. bis 15. April 2026 ergab, dass 55 % der Amerikaner sagen, dass sich ihre finanzielle Situation verschlechtert, der höchste Stand seit 2001. Patel sagte, er habe keine Informationen darüber, ob die USA außerirdische Körper aus abgestürzten UFOs geborgen hätten, und das All-Domain Anomaly Resolution Office des Pentagons habe Hunderte neuer Fälle untersucht, außerirdische Beweise jedoch nicht bestätigt.

articles.title: Rep. Tim Burchett Warns Classified UAP Briefing Contains Explosive Information That Could ‘Set the Earth on Fire’
articles.summary: Rep. Tim Burchett (R‑Tenn.), a member of the House Task Force on the Declassification of Federal Secrets, warned that classified briefings on unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAP) contain information so profound it would shock the American public and cause people to worry. In a Newsmax interview, he said he has been briefed by “just about every alphabet agency” and that full release of what he has seen would “unglue” the country, with citizens demanding immediate answers. Burchett referred to a classified session about two weeks earlier, describing the information as something that “would’ve set the Earth on fire,” but he did not disclose classified details, citing legal restrictions on members of Congress. He urged President Donald Trump—who earlier this year pledged to direct agencies to release files related to UFOs, UAP, and alien and extraterrestrial life—to push for full disclosure, adding that he is not suicidal and does not take risks amid longstanding allegations of threats to those with knowledge of the topic. Burchett has long criticized government secrecy on UAP, previously introducing the UAP Transparency Act and participating in efforts to declassify records. The article notes that former Rep. Matt Gaetz has alleged briefings on secret military programs involving hybrid breeding between captured extraterrestrials and humans, but Gaetz has not verified those claims, and the disappearance of retired Air Force Maj. Gen. William “Neil” McCasland has drawn attention due to his background in advanced aerospace research, though no evidence of foul play has been confirmed. Official Pentagon statements attribute most UAP reports to mundane explanations such as balloons, drones, or sensor artifacts, and no concrete evidence has been publicly released confirming alien‑human hybrids, recovered extraterrestrial bodies, or programs involving non‑human entities.

Er forderte Präsident Donald Trump – der Anfang des Jahres den Behörden versprach, Dateien im Zusammenhang mit UFOs, UAP sowie außerirdischem und außerirdischem Leben freizugeben – auf, auf eine vollständige Offenlegung zu drängen, und fügte hinzu, dass er nicht selbstmörderisch sei und angesichts der seit langem bestehenden Vorwürfe der Bedrohung derjenigen, die sich mit dem Thema auskennen, kein Risiko eingegangen sei. articles.title: The Rendlesham Forest mystery: ‘It’s the perfect storm of a UFO case’
articles.summary: Nick Pope, who worked for the UK Ministry of Defence from 1985 to 2006 and headed the UFO desk from 1991 to 1994, said in a 1996 interview that after reviewing evidence he believed that 5–10 % of UFO sightings could not be explained by conventional means and might be extraterrestrial craft. Pope’s research led him to focus on the Rendlesham Forest incident of 24–26 December 1980, when two American airmen reported strange blinking red, blue and white lights near RAF Bentwaters, a US base that housed nuclear missiles. The case is described by Pope as a “perfect storm” because it involved multiple military witnesses, radar confirmation, physical evidence such as ground indentations and scorch marks, and declassified documents now available at the National Archives and the MoD website. Pope co‑authored the 2014 book Encounter in Rendlesham Forest with eyewitnesses Jim Penniston and John Burroughs, who described how they investigated the lights, found a triangular craft with no landing gear, and observed a bright, static‑like burst of light that left them stunned. Penniston reported that the craft was about six and a half feet tall, had a dorsal fin and engravings resembling Egyptian hieroglyphs, and that its surface felt warm and smooth, while Burroughs was frozen in a beam of white/blue light. The incident remains unexplained, with the witnesses noting that the craft did not move when they attempted to push it and that the surrounding area showed unusual indentations, suggesting technology beyond current human capability.

de.title: Das Rätsel um den Rendlesham Forest: „Es ist der perfekte Sturm eines UFO-Falls“
de.summary: Nick Pope, der von 1985 bis 2006 für das britische Verteidigungsministerium arbeitete und von 1991 bis 1994 die UFO-Abteilung leitete, sagte 1996 in einem Interview, dass er nach Prüfung der Beweise davon ausgegangen sei, dass 5–10 % der UFO-Sichtungen nicht mit konventionellen Mitteln erklärt werden könnten und dass es sich möglicherweise um außerirdische Schiffe handele. Popes Nachforschungen führten dazu, dass er sich auf den Vorfall im Rendlesham Forest vom 24. bis 26. Dezember 1980 konzentrierte, als zwei amerikanische Flieger seltsam blinkende rote, blaue und weiße Lichter in der Nähe von RAF Bentwaters meldeten, einem US-Stützpunkt, auf dem Atomraketen stationiert waren. Der Fall wird von Pope als „perfekter Sturm“ beschrieben, da er mehrere militärische Zeugen, Radarbestätigungen, physische Beweise wie Bodeneindrücke und Brandspuren sowie freigegebene Dokumente umfasste, die jetzt im Nationalarchiv und auf der Website des Verteidigungsministeriums verfügbar sind. Pope war 2014 Co-Autor des Buches Encounter in Rendlesham Forest mit den Augenzeugen Jim Penniston und John Burroughs, die beschrieb, wie sie die Lichter untersuchten, ein dreieckiges Fahrzeug ohne Fahrwerk fanden und einen hellen, statischen Lichtausbruch beobachteten, der sie fassungslos machte. Penniston berichtete, dass das Fahrzeug etwa sechseinhalb Fuß hoch war, eine Rückenflosse und Gravuren hatte, die an Ägypter erinnerten Hieroglyphen, und dass sich seine Oberfläche warm und glatt anfühlte, während Burroughs in einem weiß-blauen Lichtstrahl erstarrt war. Der Vorfall bleibt ungeklärt, da die Zeugen feststellten, dass sich das Fahrzeug nicht bewegte, als sie versuchten, es zu schieben, und dass die Umgebung ungewöhnliche Vertiefungen aufwies, was auf eine Technologie hindeutet, die über die derzeitigen menschlichen Fähigkeiten hinausgeht.

articles.title: Conspiracy theory over UFOs and missing scientists spreads from web to White House
articles.summary: A conspiracy theory claiming that the disappearances or deaths of at least 11 U.S. scientists linked to space, defense, and nuclear research are part of a plot involving China or other state enemies and UFOs has spread from the internet to right‑wing media, mainstream press, and prompted congressional inquiry and questions from Donald Trump. The theory began with the disappearance of retired Air Force Major General William “Neil” McCasland on 27 February, who left his home in Albuquerque with his phone and glasses behind, was reported missing by his wife, and a silver alert was issued, but no trace of him has been found. Other cases cited in the theory include NASA scientist Michael David Hicks, who died in 2023; Monica Reza, who vanished while hiking in 2023; astrophysicist Carl Grillmair, who was shot on his porch; MIT physicist Nuno Loureiro, who was killed by a former classmate; chemical biologist Jason Thomas, whose remains were found in Massachusetts; and researcher Amy Eskridge, who died by suicide in 2022. Republican lawmakers James Comer and Eric Burlison sent a letter to the FBI, Department of Energy, NASA, and other agencies demanding an investigation into a “possible sinister connection” among the disappearances, citing the deaths of two Los Alamos National Laboratory workers and the alleged close professional link between Reza and McCasland. Investigators have said they are investigating every possible link, but have not confirmed any connection, and the White House has not confirmed any link between the cases. Experts such as Greg Eghigian and Susan McCasland Wilkerson have argued that the narrative is driven by a convergence of existing fears about UFOs, nuclear facilities, and state secrets, and that there is no evidence to support the claim that the scientists were abducted or targeted for their knowledge.

articles.title: Matthew James Sullivan Case: FBI Investigates Deaths of UFO-Linked Scientists Following Overdose Ruling for
articles.summary: Authorities in Virginia confirmed that former Air Force intelligence officer Matthew James Sullivan, 39, died from an accidental drug overdose after being found dead in his Falls Church home in May 2024, and the release of his autopsy results has reignited interest in the case. The toxicology report identified a fatal combination of alcohol and prescription medications—alprazolam, cyclobenzaprine, and imipramine—and medical examiners ruled the manner of death accidental. Sullivan had been preparing to testify to Congress about a secret government “crash retrieval” program and claimed to be part of a decades‑old legacy program involving the retrieval and possession of unidentified aerial phenomena. Representative Eric Burlison (R‑Mo.) has formally requested that the FBI investigate the circumstances of Sullivan’s death, describing it as “suspicious” and citing substantial questions that could have national‑security implications. The FBI has announced it is spearheading an investigation into potential connections between Sullivan and approximately 12 other scientists or specialists tied to sensitive government research who have died or gone missing, working with the Department of Energy and the Department of Defense. The House Oversight Committee, chaired by James Comer (R‑Ky.), has designated Sullivan’s death as a high‑priority matter, stating that the safety of such specialists is a matter of national security.

articles.title: UFO Files Release: Anna Paulina Luna Says First Drop Today; Requested Videos in Drop B
articles.summary: Representative Anna Paulina Luna announced that the first drop of declassified UFO files would be released at 8:00 a.m. EST on May 8, 2026, following an executive mandate for UAP transparency. Luna had previously requested 46 classified military videos from the Pentagon in March, citing whistleblower testimony and incidents near sensitive military locations, and she indicated that the requested material would appear in a second release, “Drop B.” The initial drop was expected to contain government records on unidentified anomalous phenomena, but online discussion suggested it may not include the specific military footage Luna sought, with some users anticipating a later Drop B for that content. US intelligence Director Scott D. Berrier commented that “the American people deserve truth,” while other commentators expressed cautious optimism and warned against a “big let down.” Former defense officials have cautioned that many UAP videos could have conventional explanations related to sensor limitations, aircraft, or classified military technology, and the Pentagon has not confirmed the existence or authenticity of the described footage. The disclosure effort has gained momentum after President Donald Trump said new UFO‑related files could be released “very soon,” and Luna has been communicating with officials at the Pentagon, FBI, ODNI, and the White House about disclosure. No official indication has yet been given about the scope of Friday’s release or whether any classified videos will be included in the initial batch.

summaries.accidental-congress-died-fbi-investigati: Matthew James Sullivan, a 39‑year‑old Air Force veteran who had worked for the Air Force Intelligence Agency, the National Air and Space Intelligence Center and the NSA, was found dead in his Falls Church, Virginia home on May 12 2024; the medical examiner ruled his death an accidental overdose of alcohol, alprazolam, cyclobenzaprine and imipramine. Sullivan had been scheduled to testify before Congress in November 2024 about the government’s secret UFO crash‑retrieval program and had personally seen UFOs in federal possession. Representative Eric Burlison sent a letter to FBI Director Kash Patel expressing concern that Sullivan’s sudden death raised “substantial questions” and potential foul play, citing national‑security implications. The FBI responded that it is “spearheading the effort to look for connections into the missing and deceased scientists” and is working with the Department of Energy, the Department of War, and state and local law‑enforcement partners. Retired Major General David Abba, who directed the Pentagon’s special access programs, said at Sullivan’s funeral that Sullivan “held the burden that a select few in this nation have of truly understanding what’s going on.” The article notes a pattern of other whistleblowers—such as David Grusch, David Wilcock and Amy Eskridge—who have died under circumstances the authorities have ruled accidental or self‑inflicted.
summaries.actually-data-disclosure-goes-how-pentag: A DoD‑sponsored workshop report titled “2025 UAP Workshop: Narrative Data, Infrastructures, and Analysis” was cleared for open publication on February 11, 2026 and posted to the AARO website without fanfare. The report, produced by the All‑Domain Anomaly Resolution Office, summarizes a two‑day meeting in August 2025 that brought together 40 participants from government agencies, universities, and independent research organizations. Its mandate was to design a permanent science infrastructure for studying UAP at scale, not to debate whether UAP exist. Key findings include that UAP data is fragmented across military logs, radar archives, social media, and other sources; classification is the biggest barrier; AI and machine learning are being explored but carry risks of hallucination and bias; pilot reporting stigma suppresses timely data; and fake data and AI‑generated hoaxes threaten research integrity. The workshop recommends modular metadata standards, interface control documents and APIs, a unified security solution for classified and unclassified data, and benchmarking systems, and it proposes redesigning the public reporting portal to capture free‑text descriptions, AI‑extracted structured data, and links to FAA flight tracks, weather radar, and astronomical databases. The document indicates that the U.S. government is treating UAP as a serious, data‑rich phenomenon requiring permanent scientific infrastructure and is preparing for a large‑scale public disclosure event.
summaries.accidental-agreeing-congress-died-drug-o: Matthew James Sullivan, a 39‑year‑old Air Force veteran, died on May 12, 2024 at his home in Falls Church, Va., from an accidental drug overdose involving alcohol, alprazolam, cyclobenzaprine and imipramine, according to the Northern District Office of the Chief Medical Examiner. Sullivan had agreed to testify before Congress about secret government UFO programs and was preparing to appear at a hearing scheduled for November 2024. Representative Eric Burlison (R‑Mo.) expressed grave concern over Sullivan’s death, citing “implications for national security,” and referred the case to the FBI in a letter to FBI Director Kash Patel. The FBI said it could be investigating Sullivan’s death as part of a broader effort to examine the circumstances surrounding the disappearance or death of a dozen other U.S. scientists, working with the Department of Energy, Department of War and local law enforcement. Sullivan had earned a Bronze Star for valor in Operation Enduring Freedom, worked for the Air Force Intelligence Agency, the National Air and Space Intelligence Center and the NSA, and was described by retired Major General David Abba as carrying “the burden that a select few in this nation have of truly understanding what’s going on.” Other UFO whistleblowers, such as David Grusch, have reported threats and reprisals after revealing evidence of U.S. possession of UFOs, and Grusch’s 2022 letter to the Intelligence Community Office of Inspector General included information about Sullivan’s death, though the IC OIG has stated it cannot confirm or deny any ongoing investigations.
summaries.briefed-claim-congregations-disclosure-i: Several religious leaders, including Pastor Perry Stone and evangelist Tony Merkel, claim they were privately briefed by U.S. military intelligence officers to prepare their congregations for an upcoming UAP disclosure. Stone, a Tennessee‑based Pentecostal evangelist, said in a video that a trusted associate informed him that a group of pastors was invited to hear directly from government‑connected individuals about an imminent disclosure event involving unidentified anomalous phenomena. Stone described the briefing as covering craft not allegedly part of our planet, non‑human beings, and a coordinated propaganda plan already in motion. Merkel says he has been in contact with officials for over a year, describing them as Christians in intelligence operations assigned to work within the UAP disclosure community, and that they approached him with a mission to prepare the body of Christ for what is coming. The state where the briefing took place has not been disclosed, and no government memo, official invitation, transcript, or attendee list has surfaced. The White House has not denied the claims, and Washington has remained silent, a silence the article notes is significant.
summaries.corbell-disclosure-dives-dog-fight-jerem: The trailer for “SLEEPING DOG” offers a first look at a documentary that follows investigative journalist and filmmaker Jeremy Corbell and his controversial rise in the UAP disclosure movement. Directed by Michael Lazovsky, the film has been described as a real‑life “Disclosure Day” and charts Corbell’s transition from mixed martial artist to a prominent figure pushing for transparency around unidentified aerial phenomena. It will open in select theaters on May 8, 2026, before becoming available on digital platforms on May 12. The documentary explores Corbell’s long partnership with investigative reporter George Knapp and their attempts to bring military‑filmed UAP footage and whistleblower testimony to the public eye. It promises unprecedented access and never‑before‑seen footage from Corbell’s earliest investigations, featuring figures such as astronaut Edgar Mitchell, John Lear, Bob Lazar, David Grusch, David Fravor, John B. Alexander and others involved in the UAP transparency movement. The film is produced by Jeremy Corbell, Jim Martin and Michael Lazovsky and aims to provide an intimate, high‑stakes look at one of the most polarizing subjects of the modern era.
summaries.2023-backyard-case-center-disclosure-las: On May 1, 2023 a streaking light was reported over the Southwest, and the American Meteor Society recorded sightings in Nevada, California, Arizona, and Utah. A Las Vegas family called 911 shortly afterward, claiming a crash in their backyard and describing large non‑human beings that were 8 to 10 feet tall, thin, and greenish. Police responded, body‑cam footage was captured, and the family appeared visibly shaken, while other witnesses reported a sky event. Crime‑scene reconstruction analyst Scott Roder performed a frame‑by‑frame review of the backyard video and publicly asserted that the footage showed at least two beings using a “cloaking” effect, concluding the entities were present in the real‑world environment and inviting peer review. President Donald Trump announced that UFO files would be released “very, very soon,” and members of Congress have begun openly discussing UAPs, non‑human intelligence, interdimensional theories, and the Book of Enoch. The federal government faces increasing pressure to disclose UAP records, yet NASA’s UAP study and the AARO report have found no conclusive evidence of extraterrestrial origin or verifiable extraterrestrial activity. The article argues that the Las Vegas case should be examined through a legal‑forensic review rather than dismissed or accepted on blind belief.
summaries.biologics-craft-david-grusch-human-non-w: David Grusch served 14 years in the Air Force, reaching the rank of Major and completing a combat tour in Afghanistan. He held senior intelligence positions at the National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency and the National Reconnaissance Office. Grusch represented the NRO on the Unidentified Aerial Phenomena Task Force from 2019 to 2021. In 2022 he filed a whistleblower complaint that was described as credible and urgent. In 2023 he testified before Congress. He claimed that elements of the U.S. government had thwarted Congressional oversight, withheld information from proper Executive Branch officials, and illegally concealed programs related to the recovery and reverse engineering of craft of unknown, non‑human origin.
summaries.anna-beyond-book-disclosure-enoch-frames: Rep. Anna Paulina Luna of Florida publicly suggested that the ongoing discussion about unidentified aerial phenomena may be closely connected to themes found in the Book of Enoch. She frames this link through the interdimensional hypothesis and Carl Sagan’s Flatland teaching model, arguing that both the modern UAP conversation and the ancient text describe interactions between different layers of existence. The UAP conversation, acknowledged by NASA and the Department of Defense, includes documented observations of sudden acceleration, seamless trans‑medium travel, and behavior that does not align with known propulsion systems or aerodynamics. Luna’s remarks emphasize that the Book of Enoch is not presented as scientific evidence but as an early attempt to describe encounters that lacked a clear vocabulary, mirroring the structural parallels of the interdimensional hypothesis. She does not claim that UAPs are definitively linked to the Watchers of the Book of Enoch, noting that no verified evidence currently supports such a connection. The article warns that introducing religious or historical texts into a policy‑driven discussion carries risks of misinterpretation and speculation, while acknowledging that the disclosure process is revealing complexity rather than simple answers.
summaries.confirms-delivered-director-expect-fbi-f: FBI Director Kash Patel confirmed on 6 May that the FBI has delivered its first batch of classified UFO documents to a Pentagon‑led inter‑agency committee for public release, and that the documents will be released very soon. Patel said the effort is being directed by the Department of War under Secretary Pete Hegseth across all intelligence agencies, following President Donald Trump’s February 2026 executive order to release records on unidentified anomalous phenomena and extraterrestrial matters. Trump had previously teased the release at a Turning Point USA event in Arizona, claiming the government had found “many very interesting documents.” Representative Anna Paulina Luna ordered the Pentagon to hand over 46 specific UAP video clips by 14 April 2026, but the deadline passed with no public confirmation that the videos were delivered. UFO whistleblower David Grusch warned that government forces are still working to cover up the files before release, believes disclosure will reach a tipping point in 60–90 days, and argues the focus should be on physical materials rather than videos. A NewsNation/Decision Desk HQ poll in April 2025 found 44 % of Americans believe the government is hiding UFO information, while a Gallup poll from 1–15 April 2026 reported that 55 % of Americans say their financial situation is getting worse, the highest level since 2001. Patel said he has no information on whether the US has recovered alien bodies from crashed UFOs, and the Pentagon’s All‑Domain Anomaly Resolution Office has investigated hundreds of new cases but has not confirmed extraterrestrial evidence.
summaries.briefing-burchett-classified-contains-ea: Rep. Tim Burchett (R‑Tenn.), a member of the House Task Force on the Declassification of Federal Secrets, warned that classified briefings on unidentified anomalous phenomena (UAP) contain information so profound it would shock the American public and cause people to worry. In a Newsmax interview, he said he has been briefed by “just about every alphabet agency” and that full release of what he has seen would “unglue” the country, with citizens demanding immediate answers. Burchett referred to a classified session about two weeks earlier, describing the information as something that “would’ve set the Earth on fire,” but he did not disclose classified details, citing legal restrictions on members of Congress. He urged President Donald Trump—who earlier this year pledged to direct agencies to release files related to UFOs, UAP, and alien and extraterrestrial life—to push for full disclosure, adding that he is not suicidal and does not take risks amid longstanding allegations of threats to those with knowledge of the topic. Burchett has long criticized government secrecy on UAP, previously introducing the UAP Transparency Act and participating in efforts to declassify records. The article notes that former Rep. Matt Gaetz has alleged briefings on secret military programs involving hybrid breeding between captured extraterrestrials and humans, but Gaetz has not verified those claims, and the disappearance of retired Air Force Maj. Gen. William “Neil” McCasland has drawn attention due to his background in advanced aerospace research, though no evidence of foul play has been confirmed. Official Pentagon statements attribute most UAP reports to mundane explanations such as balloons, drones, or sensor artifacts, and no concrete evidence has been publicly released confirming alien‑human hybrids, recovered extraterrestrial bodies, or programs involving non‑human entities.
summaries.case-forest-mystery-perfect-rendlesham-s: Nick Pope, who worked for the UK Ministry of Defence from 1985 to 2006 and headed the UFO desk from 1991 to 1994, said in a 1996 interview that after reviewing evidence he believed that 5–10 % of UFO sightings could not be explained by conventional means and might be extraterrestrial craft. Pope’s research led him to focus on the Rendlesham Forest incident of 24–26 December 1980, when two American airmen reported strange blinking red, blue and white lights near RAF Bentwaters, a US base that housed nuclear missiles. The case is described by Pope as a “perfect storm” because it involved multiple military witnesses, radar confirmation, physical evidence such as ground indentations and scorch marks, and declassified documents now available at the National Archives and the MoD website. Pope co‑authored the 2014 book Encounter in Rendlesham Forest with eyewitnesses Jim Penniston and John Burroughs, who described how they investigated the lights, found a triangular craft with no landing gear, and observed a bright, static‑like burst of light that left them stunned. Penniston reported that the craft was about six and a half feet tall, had a dorsal fin and engravings resembling Egyptian hieroglyphs, and that its surface felt warm and smooth, while Burroughs was frozen in a beam of white/blue light. The incident remains unexplained, with the witnesses noting that the craft did not move when they attempted to push it and that the surrounding area showed unusual indentations, suggesting technology beyond current human capability.
summaries.common-insider-interesting-missing-scien: A NewsNation article dated April 27, 2026 reports that a UFO insider claims missing scientists share interesting common ties. The claim is presented in the headline, which states that the missing scientists have “interesting common ties.” No description of the article is available in the feed metadata. The article is part of a cluster of NewsNation sources. No additional context or details are provided in the brief.
summaries.conspiracy-house-missing-scientists-spre: A conspiracy theory claiming that the disappearances or deaths of at least 11 U.S. scientists linked to space, defense, and nuclear research are part of a plot involving China or other state enemies and UFOs has spread from the internet to right‑wing media, mainstream press, and prompted congressional inquiry and questions from Donald Trump. The theory began with the disappearance of retired Air Force Major General William “Neil” McCasland on 27 February, who left his home in Albuquerque with his phone and glasses behind, was reported missing by his wife, and a silver alert was issued, but no trace of him has been found. Other cases cited in the theory include NASA scientist Michael David Hicks, who died in 2023; Monica Reza, who vanished while hiking in 2023; astrophysicist Carl Grillmair, who was shot on his porch; MIT physicist Nuno Loureiro, who was killed by a former classmate; chemical biologist Jason Thomas, whose remains were found in Massachusetts; and researcher Amy Eskridge, who died by suicide in 2022. Republican lawmakers James Comer and Eric Burlison sent a letter to the FBI, Department of Energy, NASA, and other agencies demanding an investigation into a “possible sinister connection” among the disappearances, citing the deaths of two Los Alamos National Laboratory workers and the alleged close professional link between Reza and McCasland. Investigators have said they are investigating every possible link, but have not confirmed any connection, and the White House has not confirmed any link between the cases. Experts such as Greg Eghigian and Susan McCasland Wilkerson have argued that the narrative is driven by a convergence of existing fears about UFOs, nuclear facilities, and state secrets, and that there is no evidence to support the claim that the scientists were abducted or targeted for their knowledge.

en.title: A UFO Whistleblower died of an "accidental overdose" months before he was set to testify to congress and now the FBI is now investigating
en.summary: Matthew James Sullivan, a 39‑year‑old Air Force veteran who had worked for the Air Force Intelligence Agency, the National Air and Space Intelligence Center and the NSA, was found dead in his Falls Church, Virginia home on May 12 2024; the medical examiner ruled his death an accidental overdose of alcohol, alprazolam, cyclobenzaprine and imipramine. Sullivan had been scheduled to testify before Congress in November 2024 about the government’s secret UFO crash‑retrieval program and had personally seen UFOs in federal possession. Representative Eric Burlison sent a letter to FBI Director Kash Patel expressing concern that Sullivan’s sudden death raised “substantial questions” and potential foul play, citing national‑security implications. The FBI responded that it is “spearheading the effort to look for connections into the missing and deceased scientists” and is working with the Department of Energy, the Department of War, and state and local law‑enforcement partners. Retired Major General David Abba, who directed the Pentagon’s special access programs, said at Sullivan’s funeral that Sullivan “held the burden that a select few in this nation have of truly understanding what’s going on.” The article notes a pattern of other whistleblowers—such as David Grusch, David Wilcock and Amy Eskridge—who have died under circumstances the authorities have ruled accidental or self‑inflicted.

en.title: Pentagon’s UAP Data Playbook Quietly Goes Public—And It May Reveal How Disclosure Will Actually Unfold
en.summary: A DoD‑sponsored workshop report titled “2025 UAP Workshop: Narrative Data, Infrastructures, and Analysis” was cleared for open publication on February 11, 2026 and posted to the AARO website without fanfare. The report, produced by the All‑Domain Anomaly Resolution Office, summarizes a two‑day meeting in August 2025 that brought together 40 participants from government agencies, universities, and independent research organizations. Its mandate was to design a permanent science infrastructure for studying UAP at scale, not to debate whether UAP exist. Key findings include that UAP data is fragmented across military logs, radar archives, social media, and other sources; classification is the biggest barrier; AI and machine learning are being explored but carry risks of hallucination and bias; pilot reporting stigma suppresses timely data; and fake data and AI‑generated hoaxes threaten research integrity. The workshop recommends modular metadata standards, interface control documents and APIs, a unified security solution for classified and unclassified data, and benchmarking systems, and it proposes redesigning the public reporting portal to capture free‑text descriptions, AI‑extracted structured data, and links to FAA flight tracks, weather radar, and astronomical databases. The document indicates that the U.S. government is treating UAP as a serious, data‑rich phenomenon requiring permanent scientific infrastructure and is preparing for a large‑scale public disclosure event.

de.title: Das UAP-Daten-Playbook des Pentagon wird stillschweigend veröffentlicht – und es könnte enthüllen, wie sich die Offenlegung tatsächlich entwickeln wird
de.summary: Ein vom DoD gesponserter Workshop-Bericht mit dem Titel „2025 UAP Workshop: Narrative Data, Infrastructures, and Analysis“ wurde am 11. Februar 2026 zur offenen Veröffentlichung freigegeben und ohne großes Aufsehen auf der AARO-Website veröffentlicht. Der vom All-Domain Anomaly Resolution Office erstellte Bericht fasst ein zweitägiges Treffen im August 2025 zusammen, an dem 40 Teilnehmer von Regierungsbehörden, Universitäten und unabhängigen Forschungsorganisationen teilnahmen. Sein Auftrag bestand darin, eine permanente wissenschaftliche Infrastruktur für die Untersuchung von UAP in großem Maßstab zu entwerfen, und nicht darin, darüber zu diskutieren, ob UAP existiert. Zu den wichtigsten Erkenntnissen gehört, dass UAP-Daten über Militärprotokolle, Radararchive, soziale Medien und andere Quellen fragmentiert sind; Die Klassifizierung ist das größte Hindernis. KI und maschinelles Lernen werden erforscht, bergen jedoch das Risiko von Halluzinationen und Voreingenommenheit; Das Stigma der Pilotenberichterstattung unterdrückt aktuelle Daten. und gefälschte Daten und KI-generierte Falschmeldungen bedrohen die Integrität der Forschung. Der Workshop empfiehlt modulare Metadatenstandards, Schnittstellenkontrolldokumente und APIs, eine einheitliche Sicherheitslösung für klassifizierte und nicht klassifizierte Daten sowie Benchmarking-Systeme und schlägt eine Neugestaltung des öffentlichen Berichtsportals zur Erfassung von Freitextbeschreibungen vor. KI-extrahierte strukturierte Daten und Links zu FAA-Flugspuren, Wetterradar und astronomischen Datenbanken. Aus dem Dokument geht hervor, dass die US-Regierung UAP als ernstes, datenreiches Phänomen betrachtet, das eine permanente wissenschaftliche Infrastruktur erfordert, und sich auf eine groß angelegte Offenlegungsveranstaltung vorbereitet.

The FBI said it could be investigating Sullivan’s death as part of a broader effort to examine the circumstances surrounding the disappearance or death of a dozen other U.S. scientists, working with the Department of Energy, Department of War and local law enforcement. Sullivan had earned a Bronze Star for valor in Operation Enduring Freedom, worked for the Air Force Intelligence Agency, the National Air and Space Intelligence Center and the NSA, and was described by retired Major General David Abba as carrying “the burden that a select few in this nation have of truly understanding what’s going on.” Other UFO whistleblowers, such as David Grusch, have reported threats and reprisals after revealing evidence of U.S. possession of UFOs, and Grusch’s 2022 letter to the Intelligence Community Office of Inspector General included information about Sullivan’s death, though the IC OIG has stated it cannot confirm or deny any ongoing investigations.

en.title: Multiple Pastors Claim U.S. Military Intelligence Officers Secretly Briefed Them To Prepare Their Congregations For UAP Disclosure
en.summary: Several religious leaders, including Pastor Perry Stone and evangelist Tony Merkel, claim they were privately briefed by U.S. military intelligence officers to prepare their congregations for an upcoming UAP disclosure. Stone, a Tennessee‑based Pentecostal evangelist, said in a video that a trusted associate informed him that a group of pastors was invited to hear directly from government‑connected individuals about an imminent disclosure event involving unidentified anomalous phenomena. Stone described the briefing as covering craft not allegedly part of our planet, non‑human beings, and a coordinated propaganda plan already in motion. Merkel says he has been in contact with officials for over a year, describing them as Christians in intelligence operations assigned to work within the UAP disclosure community, and that they approached him with a mission to prepare the body of Christ for what is coming. The state where the briefing took place has not been disclosed, and no government memo, official invitation, transcript, or attendee list has surfaced. The White House has not denied the claims, and Washington has remained silent, a silence the article notes is significant.

Stone, ein in Tennessee ansässiger Pfingstevangelist, sagte in einem Video, ein vertrauenswürdiger Mitarbeiter habe ihn darüber informiert, dass eine Gruppe von Pastoren eingeladen worden sei, direkt von Personen mit Regierungskontakten über eine bevorstehende Offenlegung unbekannter anomaler Phänomene zu hören. Stone beschrieb das Briefing so, dass es sich um Raumfahrzeuge handelte, die angeblich nicht Teil unseres Planeten sind, nichtmenschliche Lebewesen und einen koordinierten Propagandaplan, der bereits umgesetzt wurde. Merkel sagt, er stehe seit über einem Jahr mit Beamten in Kontakt und beschreibe sie als Christen in Geheimdienstoperationen, die innerhalb der Offenlegungsgemeinschaft der UAP arbeiten sollen, und dass sie mit der Mission an ihn herangetreten seien, den Leib Christi auf das Kommende vorzubereiten. Der Staat, in dem das Briefing stattfand, wurde nicht bekannt gegeben, und es sind weder ein Regierungsmemorandum noch eine offizielle Einladung, ein Protokoll oder eine Teilnehmerliste aufgetaucht. Das Weiße Haus hat die Behauptungen nicht bestritten und Washington hat geschwiegen, ein Schweigen, das dem Artikel zufolge bedeutsam ist.

en.title: ‘SLEEPING DOG’ Trailer Dives Into Jeremy Corbell’s Fight For UAP Disclosure
en.summary: The trailer for “SLEEPING DOG” offers a first look at a documentary that follows investigative journalist and filmmaker Jeremy Corbell and his controversial rise in the UAP disclosure movement. Directed by Michael Lazovsky, the film has been described as a real‑life “Disclosure Day” and charts Corbell’s transition from mixed martial artist to a prominent figure pushing for transparency around unidentified aerial phenomena. It will open in select theaters on May 8, 2026, before becoming available on digital platforms on May 12. The documentary explores Corbell’s long partnership with investigative reporter George Knapp and their attempts to bring military‑filmed UAP footage and whistleblower testimony to the public eye. It promises unprecedented access and never‑before‑seen footage from Corbell’s earliest investigations, featuring figures such as astronaut Edgar Mitchell, John Lear, Bob Lazar, David Grusch, David Fravor, John B. Alexander and others involved in the UAP transparency movement. The film is produced by Jeremy Corbell, Jim Martin and Michael Lazovsky and aims to provide an intimate, high‑stakes look at one of the most polarizing subjects of the modern era.

Der von Michael Lazovsky inszenierte Film wurde als „Disclosure Day“ im wirklichen Leben beschrieben und zeigt Corbells Übergang vom Mixed Martial Artist zu einer prominenten Figur, die sich für Transparenz bei unbekannten Luftphänomenen einsetzt. Es verspricht beispiellosen Zugang und nie zuvor gesehenes Filmmaterial aus Corbells frühesten Untersuchungen, mit Persönlichkeiten wie dem Astronauten Edgar Mitchell, John Lear, Bob Lazar, David Grusch, David Fravor, John B. Alexander und anderen, die an der UAP-Transparenzbewegung beteiligt sind. Der Film wurde von Jeremy Corbell, Jim Martin und Michael Lazovsky produziert und soll einen intimen, anspruchsvollen Blick auf eines der polarisierendsten Themen der Moderne werfen.

en.title: The 2023 Las Vegas Backyard UFO Case Returns To Center Stage As Washington Moves Toward Disclosure
en.summary: On May 1, 2023 a streaking light was reported over the Southwest, and the American Meteor Society recorded sightings in Nevada, California, Arizona, and Utah. A Las Vegas family called 911 shortly afterward, claiming a crash in their backyard and describing large non‑human beings that were 8 to 10 feet tall, thin, and greenish. Police responded, body‑cam footage was captured, and the family appeared visibly shaken, while other witnesses reported a sky event. Crime‑scene reconstruction analyst Scott Roder performed a frame‑by‑frame review of the backyard video and publicly asserted that the footage showed at least two beings using a “cloaking” effect, concluding the entities were present in the real‑world environment and inviting peer review. President Donald Trump announced that UFO files would be released “very, very soon,” and members of Congress have begun openly discussing UAPs, non‑human intelligence, interdimensional theories, and the Book of Enoch. The federal government faces increasing pressure to disclose UAP records, yet NASA’s UAP study and the AARO report have found no conclusive evidence of extraterrestrial origin or verifiable extraterrestrial activity. The article argues that the Las Vegas case should be examined through a legal‑forensic review rather than dismissed or accepted on blind belief.

de.title: Der Hinterhof-UFO-Fall 2023 in Las Vegas rückt wieder in den Mittelpunkt, während Washington sich der Offenlegung nähert

Die Polizei reagierte, es wurden Bodycam-Aufnahmen gemacht und die Familie wirkte sichtlich erschüttert, während andere Zeugen von einem Vorfall am Himmel berichteten. Scott Roder, Analyst für die Rekonstruktion des Tatorts, führte eine Bild-für-Bild-Überprüfung des Hinterhofvideos durch und behauptete öffentlich, dass das Filmmaterial mindestens zwei Lebewesen zeigte, die einen „Tarneffekt“ nutzten. Präsident Donald Trump kündigte an, dass UFO-Dateien „sehr, sehr bald“ veröffentlicht würden, und Kongressabgeordnete haben begonnen, offen über UAPs, nichtmenschliche Intelligenz, interdimensionale Theorien und das Buch Henoch zu diskutieren. Die Bundesregierung steht zunehmend unter Druck, UAP-Aufzeichnungen offenzulegen, doch die UAP-Studie der NASA und der AARO-Bericht haben keine schlüssigen Beweise für außerirdischen Ursprung oder nachweisbare außerirdische Aktivitäten gefunden. Der Artikel argumentiert, dass der Fall Las Vegas durch eine rechtsforensische Prüfung untersucht werden sollte, anstatt ihn aus blindem Glauben abzulehnen oder zu akzeptieren.

en.title: UAP Whistleblower David Grusch on “Non-human” Biologics & Craft
en.summary: David Grusch served 14 years in the Air Force, reaching the rank of Major and completing a combat tour in Afghanistan. He held senior intelligence positions at the National Geospatial-Intelligence Agency and the National Reconnaissance Office. Grusch represented the NRO on the Unidentified Aerial Phenomena Task Force from 2019 to 2021. In 2022 he filed a whistleblower complaint that was described as credible and urgent. In 2023 he testified before Congress. He claimed that elements of the U.S. government had thwarted Congressional oversight, withheld information from proper Executive Branch officials, and illegally concealed programs related to the recovery and reverse engineering of craft of unknown, non‑human origin.

de.title: UAP-Whistleblower David Grusch über „nichtmenschliche“ Biologika und Handwerk
